column6: candidate additions from chatgpt/claude; BL: multiple maritime news sources confirm that Greece's Tsakos Group ordered two firm LNG carriers from HD Hyundai Heavy Industries, despite official disclosures mentioning only one vessel. Reports from TradeWinds and Splash247 indicate a total agreement for two firm ships plus options, with deliveries scheduled to expand the company's fleet. For more details, visit Splash247; going with 1 vessel for now, but another could be added potentially

MISC entered shipbuilding contract with Samsung Heavy Industries on 04-May-2026 for one FSRU (170,000 cbm, GTT Mark III membrane). Contract value KRW 484.8bn ($330M). Will be chartered to Petronas Gas Berhad for 20 years for the planned Third Regasification Terminal (RGT 3) at Lumut, Perak, Malaysia. Delivery expected 1Q 2029. Sources: TheStar.com.my (Bursa filing 04-May-2026); LNG Prime; LNG Industry; iMarine; BusinessToday Malaysia. Capacity inferred at 170,000 cbm from GTT tank design press release.

column6: Samsung HI disclosed contract on 14-May-2026 for 2 LNG carriers worth KRW 750.5bn ($505.6M, ~$253M each), Oceania-region buyer in DART filing. Shipbroking sources (Splash247, IndexBox) identify the buyer as Greek George-Economou-led TMS Cardiff Gas. Delivery by end of June 2029. Backend already has 4 TMS Cardiff/Samsung hulls 2754-2757 (Aug 2025); these 2 are additional. Hull numbers TBD (likely 2758, 2759 or similar in Samsung sequence).

column6: HD KSOE disclosed on 13-May-2026 a KRW 748.5bn ($508M) order for 2 LNG carriers; described as a contract with a "North American shipping company". Trade press (LNG Prime, Splash247, TradeWinds) attributes the order to BW LNG, the Singapore-based gas-shipping arm of Bermuda-incorporated BW Group, doubling their existing 2-ship HD Samho order from Nov 2025. These ships will reportedly feature a 3-tank design (a step beyond the 4-tank H8340/H8341 already in backend). Delivery 2H 2029 / by July 2029.

column6: HD KSOE disclosed on 14-May-2026 a KRW 743.9bn ($497M) order from an "Oceania-based client" for 2 LNG carriers at HD Hyundai Heavy Industries (Ulsan). Trade press (Splash247, IndexBox, LNG Prime, TradeWinds, Riviera) attributes to London-based alternative asset manager Hayfin Capital Management. Reports also indicate Greek owner Evalend Shipping transferred 2 LNG carrier slots at HD HHI to Hayfin for 2029 delivery — these may be the same 2 hulls or additional. Delivery by end of March 2029.
